page: Amazon Web Services c5n.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8124M processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and a base clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by 21.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 2.62 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

Benchmark data shows average performance across most CPU and memory operations. The instance achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1405.39 and a multi-core score of 7392.44. Memory bandwidth is measured at 413.11 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 49.97 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core tests show weaker performance, scoring 27.94 GB/sec and 4460.0 respectively. LLM inference on a medium 7B model is poor, with prompt processing at 12.14 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5n.2xlarge offers a balanced compute-to-memory ratio for network-intensive workloads, featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. It is suitable for compute-bound applications, static web serving (averaging 6.37 GB/sec throughput), Redis database operations (7.25 million operations/sec), and software compilation. It is less suitable for large-scale memory-intensive databases or heavy LLM inference workloads due to memory capacity and multi-threaded performance constraints.
description: Amazon Web Services c5n.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8124M processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and a 3.4 GHz clock speed, paired with 21.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Lacking local storage and GPUs, the instance relies on network-attached storage and provides a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1167.0 and a static web serving throughput of 6.37 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded performance is weaker, as shown by a Geekbench multi-core score of 4460.0 and poor 7B LLM inference speeds. This instance is suitable for network-optimized compute tasks, software compilation, and Redis operations, offering a balanced resource profile for medium-scale, compute-bound workloads without requiring local storage.
